title: Tamil Alphabets (அகரமுதல)
content: Tamil uses its own script (தமிழ் லிபி). Learn vowels (உயிரெழுத்து) and consonants (மெய்யெழுத்து) below.
intro: The Tamil script has 12 vowels (uyir ezhuttu), 18 consonants (mei ezhuttu), and one special character ஃ. Below are the vowels and consonants with transliteration and usage examples.

title: Vowels and Consonants in Tamil and their pronunciation
content: Tamil script has 12 vowel letters and 18 consonant letters. Each consonant has an inherent vowel 'a' (அ).
intro: Tamil is written left to right. Vowels are uyir ezhuttu (soul letters), consonants are mei ezhuttu (body letters). Consonants are grouped: vallinam (hard), mellinam (soft), idaiyinam (medium).

title: Tamil vowel signs with consonants (உயிர்மெய்)
content: How vowels combine with consonants using vowel signs (matras).
intro: When a vowel follows a consonant in Tamil, it is written with a vowel sign. The consonant க (ka) with different vowels: கா கி கீ கு கூ கெ கே கை கொ கோ கௌ.

title: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Tamil
content: Tips to differentiate similar-sounding consonants and vowels in Tamil.
intro: Learners often confuse similar pairs. Tamil has dental vs retroflex (ந-ண, த-ட, ல-ள), and single vs double consonants (gemination). Practice with the examples below.

title: Special character ஃ (ஆய்த எழுத்து) and nasal sounds
content: The character ஃ (akku) and nasal pronunciation in Tamil.
intro: ஃ (āytha ezhuttu or akku) is neither a vowel nor a consonant. It is used in some words and for foreign sounds. Nasal sounds occur when consonants like ங, ஞ, ண, ந, ம, ன follow or combine with others.

tables.heading: Words with special and nasal sounds

title: Combining consonants in Tamil (மெய்யெழுத்து)
content: When two consonants appear together, the first is written with a pulli (்) to remove its vowel.
intro: In Tamil, consonant clusters are written by adding pulli (்) to the first consonant, then writing the second. Example: ந் + த = ந்த (nth), ட் + ட = ட்ட (ṭṭ).

title: Combining consonants with ர (r) – Part 2
content: How ர (r) combines with other consonants in Tamil.
intro: When ர follows another consonant, the first consonant gets pulli and ர is written next. Examples: க் + ர = க்ர, ப் + ர = ப்ர.

title: Combining consonants with other letters – Part 3
content: More consonant combinations in Tamil words.
intro: Tamil uses combinations like ஞ்ச (ñc), ந்த (nt), ட்ட (ṭṭ), ம்ப (mp). These appear in common words.

title: Special or separate symbols for combined consonants
content: Some combined consonants have special forms or are written in sequence.
intro: Grantha letters (ஜ, ஶ, ஷ, ஸ, ஹ) and ligatures like க்ஷ, ஶ்ரீ are used for Sanskrit and loanwords. In standard Tamil, most combinations are written with pulli + consonant.

title: Pronouns and Articles in Tamil
content: Pronouns and Articles in Tamil.
intro: Learn Tamil pronouns. Tamil has no articles like 'a', 'an', 'the'. Use the speaker buttons to hear pronunciation.

title: Using plurals to indicate respect (ஆதரார்த்த பன்மை)
content: Using plurals to indicate respect in Tamil.
intro: In Tamil, using plural forms for 'you' (நீங்கள்) and 'they' (அவர்கள்) shows respect. Singular நீ is informal.

title: Verbs in Tamil
content: Common verbs in Tamil with infinitive and usage.
intro: Tamil verb stems combine with tense suffixes. Below are common verbs in infinitive form.

title: Simple Present Tense in Tamil
content: Simple Present Tense in Tamil.
intro: Formula: Subject + verb stem + present suffix (கிற்/கின்ற). Suffix depends on person: -கிறேன் (I), -கிறாய் (you sg), -கிறான் (he), -கிறாள் (she), -கிறோம் (we), -கிறீர்கள் (you pl), -கிறார்கள் (they).

title: Simple Present Tense of "To Be" in Tamil
content: "To Be" in Tamil is இருக்க (irukka). Different forms for each pronoun.
intro: In Tamil "To Be" is இருக்க (irukka). Present forms: நான் இருக்கிறேன் (I am), நீ இருக்கிறாய் (you are), அவன் இருக்கிறான் (he is), அவள் இருக்கிறாள் (she is), நாம் இருக்கிறோம் (we are), அவர்கள் இருக்கிறார்கள் (they are).

title: Present Continuous Tense in Tamil
content: Present Continuous in Tamil – action happening now.
intro: Form: subject + verb stem + -கிற/கின்ற + இருக்க in present. E.g. நான் செய்துகொண்டிருக்கிறேன் (I am doing).

title: Simple Future Tense in Tamil
content: Simple Future Tense in Tamil.
intro: Future is formed with verb stem + -வ்/-ப் + personal suffix. E.g. நான் செய்வேன் (I will do), அவன் வருவான் (He will come).

blocks.heading: Future tense examples
rows: I will give you the book

title: Future Continuous Tense in Tamil
content: Future Continuous in Tamil.
intro: Action that will be ongoing at a future time. Form: future of main verb + கொண்டிருக்க.

title: Simple Past Tense in Tamil – Part 1 (Verbs without object)
content: Simple Past Tense – intransitive verbs.
intro: Past tense: verb stem + past suffix. நான் போனேன் (I went), அவன் வந்தான் (He came).

title: Simple Past Tense in Tamil – Part 2 (Verbs with object)
content: Simple Past Tense – transitive verbs.
intro: With object, same past stem; subject suffix agrees with subject. நான் சோறு சாப்பிட்டேன் (I ate food).

title: Simple Past Tense in Tamil – Part 3 (Past tense of "to be")
content: Past tense of "To Be" (இருக்க) in Tamil.
intro: நான் இருந்தேன் (I was), அவன் இருந்தான் (He was), அவர்கள் இருந்தார்கள் (They were).

title: Exceptional Verbs in Tamil which change in past tense form
content: Irregular past tense verbs in Tamil.
intro: Some verbs have irregular past stems. E.g. வா (come) → வந்த (came), சாப்பிடு (eat) → சாப்பிட்ட (ate).

title: Past Continuous Tense in Tamil
content: Past Continuous in Tamil.
intro: Action that was ongoing in the past. Verb + கொண்டிருக்க in past.

title: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Tamil
content: Perfect tenses in Tamil.
intro: Perfect: completed action. Form uses past participle + இருக்க in present/past/future.

title: Learn Prepositions in Tamil
content: Common prepositions and postpositions in Tamil.
intro: Tamil uses postpositions (after the noun). Common ones: -இல் (in/at), -க்கு (to), -இலிருந்து (from), -உடன் (with).

title: Preposition "TO" in Tamil
content: Using "to" (க்கு) in Tamil.
intro: Destination or recipient: noun + க்கு/கு. E.g. வீட்டுக்கு (to home), அவனுக்கு (to him).

title: Sentences with person or living things as object
content: Object markers when the object is a person.
intro: When object is a person, use -ஐ/-எ (accusative). E.g. அவனை பார்த்தேன் (I saw him).

title: Saying My/His/Her in Tamil
content: Possessives in Tamil.
intro: My = என், your (sg) = உன், his = அவன் + suffix, her = அவள் + suffix, our = எங்கள், your (pl) = உங்கள், their = அவர்கள் + suffix.

title: Basic questions and "WH" questions in Tamil
content: Question formation in Tamil.
intro: WH words: யார் (who), எது (which), எங்கே (where), எப்போது (when), ஏன் (why), எப்படி (how), எவ்வளவு (how much).

title: Negative Sentences – Present tense in Tamil
content: Negation in present tense.
intro: Use இல்லை (illa) for "to be" negation. For verbs: verb stem + -வது இல்லை or -கிறது இல்லை. E.g. நான் படிக்கவில்லை (I don't read).

title: Negative Sentences – Past tense in Tamil
content: Negation in past tense.
intro: Past negative: verb stem + -வில்லை or past form + இல்லை. E.g. நான் போகவில்லை (I didn't go).

title: Negative Sentence – Future Tense in Tamil
content: Negation in future tense.
intro: Future negative: verb stem + -மாட்ட் + person. E.g. நான் போகமாட்டேன் (I will not go).

title: Working with nouns – Gender & Plurals in Tamil
content: Gender and plural formation in Tamil.
intro: Tamil has rational (humans) vs irrational (animals, things). Plurals: -கள் for rational, -என்று/கள் for some nouns. E.g. மாணவன் → மாணவர்கள்.

title: Working with nouns – Prepositions in Tamil
content: Using nouns with postpositions.
intro: Noun + postposition. E.g. வீட்டில் (in the house), பள்ளிக்கு (to school).

title: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings
content: Distinguishing similar postpositions.
intro: Some postpositions sound or mean similarly. E.g. -க்கு (to/for), -ஆக (as/for); -இல் (in), -உள்ளே (inside).

title: Cases in Tamil (விபக்தி)
content: Grammatical cases in Tamil.
intro: Tamil has eight cases: nominative, accusative (-ஐ), instrumental (-ஆல்), dative (-க்கு), ablative (-இருந்து), genitive (-உடைய/ஐன்), locative (-இல்), vocative.

title: Commands / Imperative statements in Tamil
content: Imperative mood in Tamil.
intro: Commands: use verb stem. Polite: verb stem + -உங்கள். Negative: verb + -க்காதே / -வேண்டாம்.

title: Time related words
content: Time expressions in Tamil.
intro: Common time words: today, yesterday, tomorrow, now, morning, evening, etc.

title: Verbs indicating cause of action. Causative verb
content: Causative verbs in Tamil.
intro: Causative: someone causes another to do an action. Form: verb stem + -ப்பி/-வி. E.g. படி (read) → படிப்பி (make someone read).

blocks.heading: Causative examples
rows: to drink → to give to drink

title: Vowel harmony rules in Tamil
content: Vowel harmony in Tamil grammar.
intro: Suffix vowels may change to agree with the root vowel. E.g. front vs back vowels in verb conjugation.

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Tense – Second Style
content: Alternative perfect tense formation.
intro: Second style uses different word order or auxiliary. E.g. செய்து விட்டேன் (I have done).

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Continuous tense in Tamil
content: Perfect continuous in Tamil.
intro: Action that started in the past and continued up to a point. Form: verb + கொண்டிருக்க in perfect.

title: Difference between இல்லை (illai) and வேண்டாம் (vēṇṭām)
content: When to use illai vs vēṇṭām.
intro: இல்லை = 'is not' / 'don't have' / negative of 'to be'. வேண்டாம் = 'don't want' / 'no need' / refusal.

title: Sentence using "Going to" phrase
content: "Going to" for future intention in Tamil.
intro: Use போ (go) + infinitive or -க்கு form to express 'going to do'.

blocks.heading: Going to – examples
rows: We are going to the market
She is going to write a letter
They are going to watch a movie
I am going to Chennai tomorrow
You are going to learn Tamil
He is going to buy a car
We are going to meet them
They are going to leave early
She is going to call you
We are going to start now
He is going to tell the truth

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase
content: "Used to" (past habit) in Tamil.
intro: Past habit: verb stem + -கிற + இருந்த. அவன் இங்கே வருகிறிருந்தான் (He used to come here).

blocks.heading: Used to – examples
rows: They used to live in Madurai
I used to read a lot
We used to travel by bus
They used to visit the temple
I used to wake up early

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase – old style
content: Alternative way to say "used to" in Tamil.
intro: Same idea with different phrasing: நான் போகிறிருந்தேன் (I used to go).

blocks.heading: Used to – old style
rows: They used to come on Sundays
We used to play in the park
I used to eat rice daily

title: Sentence using "If-Then"
content: If-then conditional sentences in Tamil.
intro: என்றால் (if ... then). Or -ஆல்/-ஆனால் on verb.

blocks.heading: If-Then examples
rows: If you come, I will go
If it rains, we will stay
If you study, you will pass
If there is time, we will meet
If she comes, I will be happy
If you want, I will help
If he works hard, he will succeed
அவன் கடினமாக வேலை செய்தால் வெற்றி பெறுவான்
avaṉ kaṭiṉamāka vēlai ceytāl veṟṟi peṟuvāṉ
If they invite us, we will go
If it is true, I will believe
If you hurry, you will catch the bus
If she agrees, we can start
If I have money, I will buy it
If they come early, we can leave
If the weather is good, we will go out

title: Prepositions with Verbs
content: Verbs that take specific postpositions in Tamil.
intro: Some verbs require -ஐ, -க்கு, -இலிருந்து etc.

title: Adjectives in Tamil
content: Adjectives and their agreement in Tamil.
intro: Adjectives may agree in gender/number. பெரிய (big), சிறிய (small).

title: Using verb "Can/To be able to"
content: Expressing ability (can / be able to) in Tamil.
intro: Use -லாம் (can), or முடியும் (able): நான் செய்ய முடியும் (I can do).

blocks.heading: Can / be able to
rows: I can read and write Tamil

title: Using verb To Want/To Need
content: To want and to need in Tamil.
intro: வேண்டும் (want/need), விரும்பு (want). எனக்கு வேண்டும் (I want/need).

blocks.heading: Want / Need
rows: She wants to come with us

title: Verb "To Become/To Happen"
content: To become / to happen in Tamil.
intro: ஆகு (āku) = become, happen. அவன் டாக்டராக ஆனான் (He became a doctor).

title: Using "Should"
content: Expressing "should" (obligation/advice) in Tamil.
intro: வேண்டும் or -வேண்டும் with verb. நீ போக வேண்டும் (You should go).

title: Using "Must"/"to be have to"
content: Must / have to in Tamil.
intro: வேண்டும் (must/have to). நான் செய்ய வேண்டும் (I must do).

blocks.heading: Must / Have to
rows: She has to attend the meeting

title: Using phrase "to keep doing"
content: To keep doing (continuous repetition) in Tamil.
intro: Verb + -கிற + கொண்டிரு. அவன் பேசிக்கொண்டே இருக்கிறான் (He keeps talking).

title: Comparison and degrees of comparison
content: Comparatives and superlatives in Tamil.
intro: More: -விட (than). Most: மிகவும் or அதிகம். பெரியது (big), அதைவிட பெரியது (bigger).

title: Using verb "To Know"
content: To know in Tamil.
intro: அறி (aṟi) / தெரி (teri). எனக்கு தெரியும் (I know). அவனுக்கு தெரியாது (He doesn't know).

title: Using "Let" and "Shall we"
content: Let (permission) and Shall we (suggestion) in Tamil.
intro: விடு (let). நாம் ... செய்யலாமா? (Shall we do ...?).

title: Which-That / What-That sentences
content: Relative clauses (which/that, what) in Tamil.
intro: அது (that which), என்ன (what). வந்தவன் (the one who came).

title: Giving instructions formally
content: Formal instructions and requests in Tamil.
intro: Use -உங்கள் (plural/respectful imperative): செய்யுங்கள் (please do), வாருங்கள் (please come).

title: Using "To Like"
content: To like in Tamil.
intro: பிடி (piṭi) / இஷ்டம் (iṣṭam). எனக்கு பிடிக்கும் (I like).

title: Sentences using "would" in Tamil
content: Would (conditional/past habit) in Tamil.
intro: Past habitual or polite: -கிறிருந்த (used to) or conditional -ஆல்.

title: "To Understand" & "To come to know"
content: To understand and to come to know in Tamil.
intro: புரி (puri) understand, தெரி (teri) come to know.

blocks.heading: Understand / Come to know
rows: She came to know the truth
When did you come to know?
He came to know from her

title: Add a question tag
content: Question tags in Tamil.
intro: Add -அல்லவா? -இல்லையா? (isn't it?, right?).

blocks.heading: Question tags
rows: You have been there, haven't you?

title: Using verb "To remember" in Tamil
content: To remember in Tamil.
intro: நினைவு (memory), நினை (remember). எனக்கு நினைவிருக்கிறது (I remember).

title: Using "To Want"/"To Need" with other verbs
content: Want/Need with another verb in Tamil.
intro: Verb in -வது + வேண்டும். நான் சாப்பிட வேண்டும் (I want to eat).

blocks.heading: Want/Need + verb
rows: He wants to buy a car

title: Using "To Want" with other person
content: Want (someone else to do) in Tamil.
intro: எனக்கு அவன் வர வேண்டும் (I want him to come).

blocks.heading: Want + other person
rows: They want you to come early
I want the children to sleep

title: Using phrase "supposed to" in Tamil
content: Supposed to (expected to) in Tamil.
intro: வேண்டும் or -வேண்டியிருந்தது (was supposed to).

blocks.heading: Supposed to
rows: She is supposed to be here
I am supposed to leave early
You are supposed to tell the truth
I am supposed to be there
She was supposed to come yesterday

title: Using "To Like" with other verbs
content: Like + verb (enjoy doing) in Tamil.
intro: பிடி + verb or -வது பிடி. எனக்கு படிக்க பிடிக்கும் (I like to read).

title: Verbs which use preposition "To" with subject and verb form as per object
content: Verbs that take dative (to) and agree with object.
intro: Some verbs take -க்கு and object controls agreement.

blocks.heading: Verb + To (dative)
rows: I will send you the details

title: Short forms in spoken Tamil
content: Short forms and contractions in spoken Tamil.
intro: அது → அது, நான் → நா, இருக்கிறேன் → இருக்கேன் in casual speech.

title: Greeting, Wishes, Blessings, Slogans in Tamil
content: Greetings, wishes and blessings in Tamil.
intro: வணக்கம் (hello), நல்வாழ்த்துகள் (good wishes), நன்றி (thank you).

title: Abuse Cursing Swear-words in Tamil
content: Avoid – offensive words (listed for learning to avoid).
intro: Swear words exist in every language; learn to recognise and avoid.

title: Compound verbs / Phrases in Tamil
content: Compound verbs (verb + verb) in Tamil.
intro: செய் + விடு, எடு + கொள் etc.

title: Calling or Addressing someone in Tamil
content: How to call or address people in Tamil.
intro: அண்ணா (brother), அக்கா (sister), சார் (sir).

title: Exclamations in Tamil
content: Exclamations and interjections in Tamil.
intro: அய்யோ!, ஓ!, சூப்பர்!

title: Requesting someone – "May I", "Can you" etc.
content: Polite requests in Tamil.
intro: கொடு (give), செய்யுங்கள் (please do). எனக்கு ... கொடுக்க முடியுமா? (Can you give me ...?).

blocks.heading: Requests
rows: Can you tell me the time?
Can you show me the way?

title: Showing uncertainty using May, Might
content: May / Might (uncertainty) in Tamil.
intro: இருக்கலாம் (may be), -க்கும். அவன் வரலாம் (He may come).

title: Adjectives made from verb
content: Verbal adjectives (participles) in Tamil.
intro: Verb stem + -கிற (present), -த்த (past). வருகிற (coming), வந்த (came).

title: Adjectives related to prepositions
content: Adjectives that imply location/direction in Tamil.
intro: மேலுள்ள (upper), கீழுள்ள (lower), உள்ளுள்ள (inner).

title: Using May to express wish
content: May (wish/blessing) in Tamil.
intro: ஆகட்டும் (may it be). நீ மகிழ்ச்சியாக இரு (May you be happy).

blocks.heading: May (wish)
rows: May the New Year bring joy
May you have a good day

title: Phrase "To Make Someone do something"
content: Causative: make someone do in Tamil.
intro: செய்வி (make do), சாப்பிடுவி (make eat / feed).

blocks.heading: Make someone do
rows: I will make you do it

title: To emphasize word in Tamil
content: Emphasis and focus in Tamil.
intro: ஏ (emphasis), -ஏ (even/only). நானே (I myself), இதே (this very).

title: Generalizing words – somewhere, somehow, anything, anybody etc.
content: Indefinite pronouns (someone, somewhere, etc.) in Tamil.
intro: யாரோ (someone), எங்கோ (somewhere), ஏதோ (something).

title: "To have" to indicate possession and relation
content: To have (possession) in Tamil.
intro: Tamil uses உள்ளது (to be) with dative: எனக்கு ... உள்ளது (I have).

title: Using "To mean". How to ask meaning in Tamil
content: Asking and giving meaning of words in Tamil.
intro: அர்த்தம் (meaning). இதற்கு அர்த்தம் என்ன? (What does this mean?).

blocks.heading: Meaning
rows: What is the meaning of this word?

title: Conjunctions in Tamil – Part 1 And, But, Because, So, That
content: Conjunctions Part 1 in Tamil.
intro: மற்றும் (and), ஆனால் (but), ஏனெனில் (because), அதனால் (so), என்று (that).

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 1
rows: I think that he will come
I said that I would come

title: Conjunctions in Tamil – Part 2 As, Or, If, in case, provided
content: Conjunctions Part 2 in Tamil.
intro: அல்லது (or), -ஆல் (if), போல (as).

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 2
rows: If you come, I will go
If it rains we will stay

title: Conjunctions in Tamil – Part 3 After, then, As far as, As long as
content: Conjunctions Part 3 in Tamil.
intro: பிறகு (then), அதன் பின் (after).

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 3
rows: As long as you are here

title: Conjunctions in Tamil Part 4 – As soon as, Once, Rather than, So that
content: Conjunctions Part 4 in Tamil.
intro: வேகமாக (as soon as), அதனால் (so that).

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 4
rows: As soon as I saw him

title: List of Conjunctions in Tamil
content: List of common conjunctions in Tamil.
intro: Summary of conjunctions.

title: Combining sentences – change in form of verb இருக்க (iru)
content: Combining two sentences using verb இருக்க in Tamil.
intro: When combining clauses, இருக்க may change form.

blocks.heading: Combining with இருக்க
rows: He is there and I am here
அவன் அங்கே இருக்கிறான், நான் இங்கே இருக்கிறேன்
avaṉ aṅkē irukkiṟāṉ, nāṉ iṅkē irukkiṟēṉ
She was at home and we were outside
அவள் வீட்டில் இருந்தாள், நாம் வெளியே இருந்தோம்
avaḷ vīṭṭil iruntāḷ, nām veḷiyē iruntōm
There is a book and there is a pen
He is here while she is there
அவன் இங்கே இருக்கிறான், அவள் அங்கே இருக்கிறாள்
avaṉ iṅkē irukkiṟāṉ, avaḷ aṅkē irukkiṟāḷ
We were happy and they were sad
நாம் மகிழ்ச்சியாக இருந்தோம், அவர்கள் சோகமாக இருந்தார்கள்
nām makiḻcciyāka iruntōm, avarkaḷ cōkamāka iruntārkaḷ
There being no bus, we walked

title: Compound verbs in Tamil
content: Compound verbs (two verbs together) in Tamil.
intro: செய்து + விடு, எடுத்து + கொள் etc.

title: Active Passive voice in Tamil
content: Active and passive voice in Tamil.
intro: Passive: object becomes subject, verb in passive form. அவனால் செய்யப்பட்டது (It was done by him).

blocks.heading: Active / Passive
rows: The letter was written by me
The glass was broken by him
The house was built by them
The wall was painted by us
The door was opened by him

title: Active Passive voice in Tamil – Style 2
content: Alternative passive construction in Tamil.
intro: Different word order or -ப்படு passive.

title: Using "To Feel" & "To think"
content: To feel and to think in Tamil.
intro: உணர் (feel), யோசி (think). எனக்கு ... உணர்வு (I feel ...).

title: Idioms and Phrases in Tamil / different sentence formation

intro: தமிழ் முதுமொழிகள் (idioms).

blocks.heading: Idioms
rows: to hit the nail on the head
to be in the same boat
to add fuel to the fire
where there is a will there is a way
to kill two birds with one stone
when in Rome do as the Romans do
எந்த ஊருக்கு போனாலும் அந்த ஊர் வழக்கம்
enta ūrukku pōṉālum anta ūr vaḻakkam

title: Idioms and Phrases in Tamil Part 2
content: More idioms and phrases in Tamil.
intro: Additional common phrases.

blocks.heading: Idioms Part 2
rows: all is well that ends well
every cloud has a silver lining
a stitch in time saves nine
don't judge a book by its cover
good things come to those who wait

title: Sentences using "If-Then" Style 2
content: If-Then conditional – alternative style in Tamil.
intro: -ஆல்/-ஆனால் on verb. போனால் (if [you] go).

blocks.heading: If-Then Style 2
rows: If you go, I will come
If she agrees, we will start
If it is true, I will believe
If you want, I can help
If he works hard, he will succeed
அவன் கடினமாக வேலை செய்தால் வெற்றி பெறுவான்
avaṉ kaṭiṉamāka vēlai ceytāl veṟṟi peṟuvāṉ
If we hurry, we will catch the bus
If she calls, tell her I am busy
அவள் அழைத்தால் நான் பிஸி என்று சொல்
avaḷ aḻaittāl nāṉ pisi eṉṟu col
If they invite us, we will go
If the weather is good, we will go out
If I have time, I will visit
If you don't mind, I will sit here
If he doesn't come, we will leave
If she says yes, we can proceed

title: Tamil practice session – prepositions part 3
content: More preposition usage in context.
intro: இடைச் சொற்கள் – மேலும் வாக்கியங்கள்.

blocks.heading: Prepositions practice 3
rows: Without you I cannot do it
Despite the rain we went out
Because of you I am happy
Instead of coffee I had tea
I will do it for you
I work from nine to five
ஒன்பது முதல் ஐந்து வரை வேலை செய்கிறேன்
oṉpatu mutal aintu varai vēlai ceykiṟēṉ
The answer is in the book

title: Tamil jokes – Learn Tamil in a fun way
content: Simple jokes and humour in Tamil.
intro: நகை, நையாண்டி – தமிழில்.

blocks.heading: Jokes and fun phrases
rows: Why did the book go to the doctor? It had too many pages!
புத்தகம் ஏன் டாக்டரிடம் போனது? அதற்கு பக்கங்கள் அதிகம்!
puttakam ēṉ ṭākṭariṭam pōṉatu? ataṟku pakkaṅkaḷ atikam!
I am not lazy, I am on energy saving mode
நான் சோம்பேறி அல்ல, சக்தி சேமிப்பு மோடில் இருக்கிறேன்
nāṉ cōmpēṟi alla, cakti cēmippu mōṭil irukkiṟēṉ
Teacher: What is the full form of OK? Student: Only Tamil!
ஆசிரியர்: ஓகேயின் முழு வடிவம்? மாணவர்: ஓன்லி தமிழ்!
āciriyar: ōkēyiṉ muḻu vaṭivam? māṇavar: ōṉli tamiḻ!
Life is short, smile while you still have teeth
A day without laughter is a day wasted
Why do we never tell secrets in the garden? Because the potatoes have eyes!
ஏன் தோட்டத்தில் ரகசியம் சொல்ல மாட்டோம்? உருளைக்கிழங்குக்கு கண் உள்ளது!
ēṉ tōṭṭattil rakaciyam colla māṭṭōm? uruḷaikkiḻaṅkukku kaṇ uḷḷatu!
I am not short, I am concentrated awesome

title: Daily Tamil reading practice – jokes, thoughts, quotes
content: Short quotes and thoughts in Tamil for reading practice.
intro: மேற்கோள்கள், சிந்தனைகள்.

blocks.heading: Quotes and thoughts
rows: Where there is a will there is a way
Success comes to those who try
The early bird catches the worm
Reading is to the mind what exercise is to the body

title: Simple Tamil sentences – My experience with Social Media
content: Talking about social media in Tamil.
intro: சமூக ஊடகம், ஃபேஸ்புக், வாட்ஸ்அப்.

blocks.heading: Social media – sentences
rows: I will send you on WhatsApp
I have many followers on Instagram
I spend two hours on phone
Do not believe everything on the internet

title: Conversation about the eclipse
content: Talking about solar/lunar eclipse in Tamil.
intro: கிரகணம், சூரிய கிரகணம், சந்திர கிரகணம்.

blocks.heading: Eclipse – sentences
rows: There is a solar eclipse today
Do not look at the sun directly
The eclipse will start at nine
We should not eat during eclipse
The lunar eclipse is at night
I have never seen an eclipse
The eclipse lasts for an hour
In olden days people were afraid of eclipse
We will watch it on TV

title: International Women's Day
content: Sentences about Women's Day and respect for women.
intro: பன்னாட்டு மகளிர் தினம், மார்ச் 8.

blocks.heading: Women's Day – sentences
rows: International Women's Day is on March 8
Thank you to all the women in my life
Happy Women's Day to my sister

title: Simple Tamil conversation – Quarrel at home
content: Expressing disagreement and resolving at home.
intro: வாதம், சண்டை – வீட்டில்.

blocks.heading: Quarrel / disagreement – sentences
rows: I am sorry for what I said
I did not mean to hurt you
I love you no matter what
